{{日本の氏族
|家名 = Iku no Kuni no Miyatsuko
|家紋 = <!-- family crest image -->
|家紋名称 =
|本姓 =
|家祖 = {{ill|Toshima-no-Mikoto|ja|豊嶋命}}
|種別 = {{ill|Tenson|ja|天孫}}
|出身地 = {{ill|Aki Province|ja|安芸国}}
|根拠地 = Iku Province (later {{ill|Mutsu Province|ja|陸奥国}})
|人物 = See [[#People|People]]
|支流 = {{ill|Aki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|阿岐国造}}<br />{{ill|Shirakawa no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|白河国造}}<br />{{ill|Asaka no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|阿尺国造}} ({{ill|Hasetsukabe-no-Atai|ja|丈部氏|lt=Hasetsukabe-no-Atai|lt_ja=丈部直}})<br />{{ill|Shineha no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|染羽国造}}<br />{{ill|Shinobu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|信夫国造}}<br />{{ill|Omoi no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|思国造}}
}}
{{nihongo|'''Iku no Kuni no Miyatsuko'''|伊久国造|''iku no kuni no miyatsuko'', ''iku kokuzō''}} was a {{ill|kuni no miyatsuko|ja|国造}} who ruled the region that later became part of {{ill|Mutsu Province|ja|陸奥国}} ('''[[#Territory|Iku Province]]''').

== Overview ==
=== Name ===
Written '''Iku no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' in the "Kokuzō Hongi" of the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}''.

=== Ancestry ===
* According to the "Kokuzō Hongi" of the ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'', during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇}}, '''{{ill|Toshima-no-Mikoto|ja|豊嶋命}}''' (''Toshima-no-Mikoto''), a 10th-generation descendant sharing the same ancestor as the {{ill|Aki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|阿岐国造}}, was appointed kuni no miyatsuko.

=== Clan ===
The clan that served as kuni no miyatsuko is unknown.

== Base ==
The kuni no miyatsuko's base (seat of government) is said to have been in {{ill|Kakuda, Miyagi|ja|角田市|lt=Kakuda City|lt_ja=角田市}}; many {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} — including {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳}} and {{ill|round kofun|ja|円墳}} — are found in Kakuda City and the neighboring {{ill|Marumori, Miyagi|ja|丸森町|lt=Marumori-machi|lt_ja=丸森町}}.

=== Territory ===
The kuni no miyatsuko's territory was the region then known as '''Iku Province''', corresponding to the later '''{{ill|Igu District|ja|伊具郡}}''' of {{ill|Mutsu Province|ja|陸奥国}} — present-day {{ill|Kakuda, Miyagi|ja|角田市|lt=Kakuda City|lt_ja=角田市}} and {{ill|Marumori, Miyagi|ja|丸森町|lt=Marumori-machi|lt_ja=丸森町}}.

== Tutelary Shrine ==
The kuni no miyatsuko's tutelary shrine is unknown. Although not listed in the ''{{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}'', {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Kakuda)|ja|諏訪神社 (角田市)|lt=Suwa Shrine|lt_ja=諏訪神社}} (in Sakura, {{ill|Kakuda, Miyagi|ja|角田市|lt=Kakuda City|lt_ja=角田市}}, {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県}}) is said to have enshrined Toshima-no-Mikoto of the Iku no Kuni no Miyatsuko, and to have been called Sakura (Sakura) Shrine.<ref>"[https://www.miyagi-jinjacho.or.jp/jinja-search/detail.php?code=310010241 Suwa Shrine]", Miyagi Prefecture Jinjachō official website.</ref>

=== Tombs ===
* '''{{ill|Chōsenji Kofun No. 4|ja|長泉寺4号墳}}''' (''chōsenji yongōfun'')
*: One of the Chōsenji Kofun Cluster, a {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳}} 74 metres in total length, built in the middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.
* '''{{ill|Yoshinouchi Kofun|ja|吉ノ内古墳}}''' (''yoshinouchi kofun'')
*: One of the Yokokura Kofun Cluster, a keyhole-shaped kofun 66 metres in total length, built in the middle {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}}.

== Notes ==
{{notelist}}
{{Reflist}}

== See also ==
* {{ill|Tōsandō|ja|東山道}}

{{DEFAULTSORT:いくのくにのみやつこ}}
[[Category:陸奥国]]
[[Category:宮城県の歴史]]
[[Category:Kuni no miyatsuko]]
[[ja:伊久国造]]
